scope: Phase 0 canonicity survey of the ET (Ethiopian) backlog, 32 texts, conducted as the first phase of the broader ET-AR-SY/Odes review sequence (Phase 0 canonicity -> Phase 1 mechanical sweeps -> Phase 2 source-availability triage -> Phase 3 full audits -> Phase 4 punt list). Companion to the AR/SY/Odes canonicity survey recorded the same review cycle, which found and removed three fabricated/non-canonical items (Laodiceans, Prayer of Apollonius, four Odes/Canticles stubs -- see their own registry files).
finding: Unlike the AR/SY/Odes survey, nothing in the ET backlog is fabricated. Every one of the 32 texts maps to a real, historically attested category. No removals resulted from this survey.

core81BookCanon.description: Texts belonging to the Ethiopian Orthodox Tewahedo Church's broader 81-book biblical canon.
core81BookCanon.note: Rest of Baruch and Rest of Jeremiah together constitute 4 Baruch / Paralipomena of Jeremiah.

ninefoldBroaderCanonBooks.description: The nine broader-canon books recognized within the wider (not strictly 81-book) Ethiopian tradition.
ninefoldBroaderCanonBooks.note: The 8 Clement/Qalemcentos files together represent a single canonical work, 'Ethiopic Clement,' divided into 7-8 parts. Sirate Tsion, Tizaz, Admonitions, and Abtilis together constitute the complete Sinodos. 'Admonitions' self-identifies internally as 'Gitsiw' -- name recorded for cross-reference, not a discrepancy.
ninefoldBroaderCanonBooks.openQuestion: This category determination is about the TEXT TYPE being real and attested -- it does NOT resolve whether this specific corpus's content is an authentic rendering of that text type. That is a separate, still-open content-accuracy question, already flagged red on the dashboard and unaffected by this survey.

churchOrderLegalCode.description: Broader-canon-adjacent Church Order text -- historically significant, not scripture.
churchOrderLegalCode.note: A legal code, historically functioning as Ethiopia's constitution -- same category as the Kebra Nagast.

misidentifiedTitle.description: Text whose stored title was misleading relative to its actual content.
misidentifiedTitle.note: Self-identifies internally (per its own metadata) as the proceedings/canons of the Council of Nicaea. Title corrected in understanding, not renamed in this survey -- a Phase 2/3 item if renaming is warranted.

historicallyCanonicalKeptByDecision.description: Texts historically claimed as canonical within parts of the Ethiopian tradition, but not included in the current strict 81-book list per modern scholarship (Cowley 1974).

decision.action: KEEP, labeled honestly as historically-claimed-but-not-strict-81-book rather than removed.
decision.precedent: Same basis this project already keeps Odes of Solomon (SY) -- genuine historical/theological significance with real, verified or verifiable content is sufficient grounds to keep, provided the canonical status is stated accurately rather than glossed over.

devotionalLiturgicalNotScripture.description: Devotional or liturgical texts, real and attested, but not scripture in any sense -- kept as devotional/liturgical content, not miscategorized as biblical.

flaggedForPhase2.reason: Title not yet independently confirmed as of this survey. Devotional/liturgical categorization is a working assumption pending Phase 2 source-availability triage, not a settled finding.

note: This is a canonicity-and-categorization survey only -- it does not certify content accuracy for any of these 32 texts. Content-accuracy verification (Phase 3 of the sequencing plan) remains entirely separate and, as of this recording, not yet begun for any text in this list except Book of Jubilees (see its own GREEN_NOTES entry and the two 2026-07-16 remediation ledger entries, unrelated to and independent of this canonicity survey).
